From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Borough of Munich, Germany

**Sendling-Westpark** (German pronunciation: [[ˈzɛntlɪŋ ˈvɛstpaʁk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German")) is the 7th borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/14/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_07_%28Karte%29_-_Sendling_-_Westpark.png/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_07_%28Karte%29_-_Sendling_-_Westpark.png)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_07_(Karte)_-_Sendling_-_Westpark.png)

Borough 7 - **Sendling-Westpark**: Location in Munich

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sendling-Westpark&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Location")]

Sendling-Westpark is located south west of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") and expands into the North/South extension from the 8th borough [Schwanthalerhöhe](/wiki/Schwanthalerh%C3%B6he "Schwanthalerhöhe") as far as Obersendling ([Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln](/wiki/Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln "Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln")). The eastern border contains the [S-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_S-Bahn "Munich S-Bahn") line to [Wolfratshausen](/wiki/Wolfratshausen "Wolfratshausen"), which connects borough 6 [Sendling](/wiki/Sendling "Sendling"). *Fürstenriederstrasse* and *Westendstrasse* form the border in the west, beyond which lie boroughs 20 [Hadern](/wiki/Hadern "Hadern") and 25 [Laim](/wiki/Laim "Laim").

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sendling-Westpark&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Description")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/Westpark_Muenchen-3.jpg/250px-Westpark_Muenchen-3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Westpark_Muenchen-3.jpg)

[Westpark](/wiki/Westpark_(Munich) "Westpark (Munich)")

Due to an influx of immigrants, the population count doubled between 1950 and 1995; nevertheless, the amount of foreign residents is well below the average population of the area. In the southern part, accommodation typically consists of [detached](/wiki/Detached_house "Detached house") and [semi-detached houses](/wiki/Semi-detached_house "Semi-detached house") built in the Interwar period. In the vicinity of the main traffic arteries, blocks of flats were built after 1948.

Important employers and facilities in the borough include the *Städtische Altenheim St. Josef* ("St Josef Home for the Elderly"), the *Lebenshilfe Werkstatt für Behinderte* ("Workshop for Disabled People"), the *Integrationszentrum für Cerebralparesen* ("Integration Centre for Cerebral Palsy") the *Bayerische Landesschule für Gehörlose* ("Bavarian State School for the Deaf"), the headquarters of the [ADAC](/wiki/ADAC "ADAC") and [TÜV](/wiki/T%C3%9CV "TÜV"). The Construction and metalworking industry employs a large portion of people in the borough as well.

Several important motorways pass through the borough, notably the A 96 (Munich-[Lindau](/wiki/Lindau "Lindau")) and A 95 (Munich-[Garmisch](/wiki/Garmisch "Garmisch")) [Autobahnen](/wiki/Autobahn "Autobahn") which connect to the "[Mittlerer Ring](/wiki/Mittlerer_Ring "Mittlerer Ring")", Munich's inner city ring road system. This has brought about concerns over the volume of traffic and environmental issues. The borough's face was altered by extensive construction and landscaping work for the 1983 IGA display [Westpark](/wiki/Westpark_(Munich) "Westpark (Munich)"), complete with provisions for open air cinema, theatre and concerts, culinary delights, a [Buddhist temple](/wiki/Buddhist_temple "Buddhist temple"), play areas and other amenities.

Located in the north of the Westpark is the *[Rudi-Sedlmayer-Halle](/wiki/Rudi-Sedlmayer-Halle "Rudi-Sedlmayer-Halle")* (Rudi Sedlmayer Hall) where cultural and sporting events previously took place. The hall is currently the home venue of the FC Bayern Munich Basketball club and has been rebranded as Audi Dome.
